text:
Market today: Mixed macro mood - Tesla cratering 7% on sentiment despite good deliveries signals the market is punishing tech/growth on any excuse right now. Your chip/tech heavy book is directly in the crossfire when sentiment flips like this.

Your holdings: CRWD is screaming for attention - down 72.65% and your stop is $590 on a $196 stock, that stop is completely detached from reality and useless. Fix that immediately. SOXL is down 24.62% and already below its $200 stop - you should have been out already on a 3X leveraged ETF, margin makes this bleed fast. SPAL is down 13.44% and sitting just above its $21.38 stop - one bad tick and it triggers.

Watch: Whether SOXL holds the scanner entry zone ($192.05-192.24) - a break below $190 stop confirms more pain. Watch if broad tech selling from Tesla's drop bleeds into SMH and QQQ.

Bottom line: Stop management is broken on CRWD and SOXL - fix those before looking at anything new.
plays: CRWD: Your stop of $590 is nonsensical at $196 price - reset a real stop immediately, scanner shows $189.24 as the structural level to respect or exit.
SOXL: Already below your $200 stop - either exit now or acknowledge you've overridden the stop; scanner entry $192.05-192.24 stop $190.04 is your last line if staying.
No new buys today - your two SELL positions need triage first; adding exposure on margin while CRWD and SOXL are bleeding is the wrong move.
